user_outcome: `xai/` is a first-class driver prefix: `xai/grok-4.5` (and other xAI models) resolves natively through the driver registry to xAI's OpenAI-compatible API using XAI_API_KEY, declaring implementer/verifier/captain roles like the other in-process prefixes — so the loop can be driven on Grok directly (`--implementer-model xai/grok-4.5`) instead of only via `openrouter/x-ai/grok-4.5`. `sworn capabilities` lists the xai driver as available when XAI_API_KEY is present.
rationale: From the 2026-07-11 dogfood: Brad wanted to drive the loop with Grok 4.5, but `sworn capabilities` showed no `xai/` prefix — grok is only reachable via `openrouter/x-ai/grok-4.5`. xAI's API is OpenAI-compatible (chat/completions + structured output), so a native driver is a provider-config + registry-registration addition, mirroring the existing in-process prefixes (deepseek/, groq/, mistral/, openrouter/) served by oai-inprocess. Grounding: internal/driver/registry (S05-driver-registry, driver-contract release) has the explicit prefix→driver table; internal/model resolves in-process clients (model.NewClient / FromEnv) by prefix; the S09 catalog (`sworn models`) enumerates providers. Add xai/ to the oai-inprocess (or oai-responses-inprocess if xAI's structured-output path matches Responses) registration with the xAI base URL + XAI_API_KEY.
in_scope: Register the `xai/` prefix in the driver registry (internal/driver/registry) → the in-process oai driver, declaring implementer/verifier/captain roles (matching the other in-process prefixes)

Provider config: internal/model resolves the xai/ prefix to xAI's OpenAI-compatible endpoint (base URL) using XAI_API_KEY (via FromEnv), including the structured-output path (ChatStructured) so xai/ models can serve verifier/captain
`sworn capabilities` reports the xai driver: prefix xai/, roles, available when XAI_API_KEY present (no dispatch)
The S09 catalog (`sworn models --provider xai`) enumerates xAI models where the provider exposes a models/list endpoint (fail-closed unknown otherwise), consistent with the other providers

Add xAI model pricing entries to the pricing catalog where known (the dogfood showed grok-4.5 unpriced → CostSource=unknown; at minimum grok-4.5) so honest cost is a real number, not unknown, for the native driver
Tests: registry resolves xai/grok-4.5 to the in-process driver with the right roles; capabilities lists it; a no-dispatch availability check keys on XAI_API_KEY
out_of_scope: A bespoke xAI SDK — xAI is OpenAI-compatible; reuse the oai in-process client (net/http + encoding/json), no provider SDK (ADR-0007)
Deprecating or changing the openrouter/x-ai/grok-4.5 route — it stays; xai/ is an additional native path
Exhaustive xAI model catalog curation — enumerate what the provider exposes, fail-closed on unknowns (S09 pattern)

risks.description: xAI's structured-output / tool-calling wire format diverges from OpenAI's, so verifier/captain (which need StructuredOutput) fail on xai/ even though chat works.
risks.mitigation: Verify xAI's structured-output support at design; if it matches the Responses or chat/completions structured path, route accordingly; if a quirk exists, contain it in the driver (the driver-contract's whole point) and declare the capability honestly (roles reflect what actually works).

risks.description: The availability check dispatches or leaks the key.
risks.mitigation: Availability is credential-presence only (XAI_API_KEY set), no dispatch — the S09 fail-closed no-dispatch pattern.

effort_complexity.rationale: Known-shape provider addition (xAI is OpenAI-compatible) mirroring existing in-process prefixes — registry registration + provider config + capabilities/catalog wiring + pricing entries. Breadth across a few files, low depth. The one unknown (structured-output wire quirk) is contained by the driver contract. Re-assess at design_review.

acceptance_criteria.text: When the driver registry resolves `xai/grok-4.5` for an implementer/verifier/captain role, it SHALL return the in-process driver configured for xAI's OpenAI-compatible endpoint using XAI_API_KEY — proven by a registry test asserting the resolution and declared roles.

When `sworn capabilities` runs with XAI_API_KEY present, it SHALL list the xai driver (prefix xai/, roles, available) without dispatching; when the key is absent it SHALL list xai/ as unavailable — proven by a capabilities test on both conditions.

When `xai/grok-4.5` serves the verifier or captain role, it SHALL emit schema-constrained structured output (ChatStructured) that validates — proven by a test through the model StructuredOutput path (httptest, no live dispatch); if xAI's structured path has a quirk it SHALL be contained in the driver and the declared roles SHALL reflect what actually works.

`sworn models --provider xai` SHALL enumerate xAI models from the provider's list endpoint (fail-closed unknown where metadata is absent), at least one known xAI model SHALL carry a pricing entry so honest cost is a real number, and `go build ./...` + `go test ./internal/driver/... ./internal/model/... ./cmd/sworn/...` SHALL pass.
